degraded: Per-layer test counts are collection-derived, not run-derived: run_test_suite.py writes its JUnit reports into a TemporaryDirectory that is removed on exit, so exact per-test pass counts from the F0 run could not be recovered. The counts above were re-derived with the runner's own command shape (uv run --with pytest --with pytest-mock pytest <unit> -q --collect-only), post-deselection. What F0 proved directly is the verdict: 18/18 units PASS, exit 0, 2.2 min.

touches_build enforced the performance test layer; it ran and opted out legitimately. performance_check.py --profile-path shared/profiles/python-plugin-monorepo.json exits 0 with both legs skipped ("profile opts out (testing.performance.enabled is false)") because this monorepo is a Python plugin framework with no web surface (dev_url null). The gate is structurally inapplicable here rather than passing on measurement.

setup_iterate_worktree reported sweep-outbox invalid: protected_status_unplaceable for trg-16d374e1 and trg-b1dda91d — appends exist in local main's tracked log but are not reachable from origin/main, which this worktree branched from. Pre-existing condition caused by 7 unpushed chore(triage) fold commits on local main; not caused by and not in scope for this iterate. Worktree creation itself succeeded (action: created).

surface_verification.justification: No startable application surface in this repo (python-plugin-monorepo profile, dev_url null, testing.performance opted out). The changed artifact is a build-time dependency manifest for the Lighthouse perf runner, which no pytest exercises: test_performance_check_integration.py fakes the Lighthouse leg via SHIPWRIGHT_PERF_LHCI_FAKE and never installs node_modules, so a pytest "cli" surface would be a vacuous pass. The artifact was instead driven directly: npm ci clean-install from the new lockfile (200 packages, integrity valid, brace-expansion 2.1.2, exactly one copy in the installed tree) and "node lighthouse-runner.mjs" returning its documented no-URL contract (exit 2, lighthouse-runner.mjs:17-21). Both recorded as tested rows in the test-completeness ledger.

behaviors.behavior: The perf-runner lockfile no longer resolves brace-expansion into the CVE-2026-13149 vulnerable range (>=2.0.0, <2.1.2)
behaviors.evidence: npm ci from the new lockfile installs brace-expansion 2.1.2; tree sweep found exactly ONE brace-expansion directory among the 200 installed packages, at 2.1.2 — no nested duplicate left at a vulnerable version

behaviors.behavior: The edited lockfile stays internally consistent and installable (dependency tree resolves, every integrity hash validates)
behaviors.evidence: npm ci in the worktree perf/ dir: "added 200 packages in 5s", exit 0 — a from-scratch resolve that validates the new brace-expansion 2.1.2 sha512 integrity hash. This is also the producer->disk->consumer round-trip for the changed serialized format (npm wrote it via --package-lock-only, npm read it back via ci)

behaviors.behavior: The Lighthouse perf runner still loads and honors its CLI contract under the new dependency tree
behaviors.evidence: node lighthouse-runner.mjs (no args) returns {"error":"no URL argument"} and exit 2 — exactly the documented contract at lighthouse-runner.mjs:17-21, proving module resolution succeeds through the rebuilt tree

behaviors.behavior: performance_check.py behavior is unchanged by the dependency bump
The bump introduces no regression elsewhere in the monorepo
behaviors.evidence: F0 full suite — 18/18 units PASS, exit code 0, 2.2 min

behaviors.behavior: The Trivy SCA scanner agrees the finding is resolved and code-scanning alert #1275 closes
behaviors.reason: Pinned by the pre-existing Trivy SCA leg in .github/workflows/security.yml, which scans this exact lockfile on every push to main and is the producer that raised alert #1275. Re-asserting the version floor in a bespoke pytest would duplicate the scanner and rot at the next advisory.
